title: <name> and the Secret of the Rain Drops

pages.text: The first monsoon clouds rolled over <name>’s town in Kerala. The air smelled of wet earth and fresh mango leaves. “Amma! It’s raining!” <name> shouted, running out into the courtyard. Raindrops splashed on her hands, her cheeks, and her hair.
Suddenly, one shiny drop landed on her palm — and blinked! “Hello there!” said the drop in a tiny voice. “I’m Boondi, a traveling raindrop!” <name> gasped. “You can talk?”

“Of course! I’ve been everywhere,” Boondi said proudly. “I was a river once. Then the sun warmed me up, and poof! — I turned into mist and floated into the sky.” “Wow!” said <name>. “So that’s how clouds are made?”

Boondi nodded, twirling on her fingertip. “Yes! When many tiny drops like me gather together, we become clouds. We float until we’re heavy, and then we fall as rain again. That’s called precipitation!” They both laughed as they danced around the puddles.

Boondi pointed up. “See those gray clouds? They’re full of my friends. When we land on fields, plants drink us. When we reach rivers, fish thank us. We help everything grow!” <name> looked at the bright green banana leaves glistening in the rain. “So every drop of water has a job!”

“Exactly!” said Boondi, winking. “And we never disappear. We just travel — from oceans to clouds, to rivers, to you!” Thunder rumbled softly, and the rain slowed. “Time for me to go,” said Boondi. “Maybe next time, you’ll see me in a puddle or your drinking glass!”

<name> smiled. “Goodbye, Boondi! I’ll never waste water again.” As the clouds drifted away and a rainbow spread across the sky, <name> whispered, “Every drop is a tiny traveler — and every traveler matters.”
